name: CLI Entry Point
description: Argparse-based CLI providing four subcommands (review, trust, history, adopt) as a thin transport layer over shared core modules. Manages async event loop lifecycle, maps pipeline results to process exit codes (0=pass, 1=warn, 2=block, 3=error), emits Chronicler events at stage boundaries, and embeds PACT keys for Sentinel attribution. All business logic is delegated to intake, circuit, assessor, reporter, chronicle, and learner modules — the CLI never inspects findings, computes scores, or formats reports directly.

types.name: CliExitCode
types.description: CLI process exit codes mapping to review outcomes.

types.name: SubcommandName
types.description: Valid CLI subcommand identifiers used as dispatch keys.

types.name: CliArgs

fields.name: diff_file
fields.description: Path to diff file (review subcommand only)

fields.name: config_path
fields.description: Override path to YAML config file. Defaults to XDG config or cwd/.exemplar.yml

fields.name: output_format
fields.description: Report output format. Only used by review subcommand.

fields.name: debug
fields.description: Enable verbose diagnostic output to stderr

types.description: Parsed and validated CLI arguments after argparse processing. Frozen Pydantic model wrapping argparse.Namespace fields into typed structure.

types.name: FilePath

validators.error_message: File path must be between 1 and 4096 characters
File path must not contain null bytes

types.description: A validated filesystem path string. Must be non-empty when required and contain only valid path characters.

types.name: CliResult

fields.name: exit_code
fields.description: Semantic exit code to return from process

fields.name: stdout_output
fields.description: Formatted output to write to stdout. Empty string means no output.

fields.name: stderr_output
fields.description: Diagnostic or error messages to write to stderr. Empty string means no diagnostics.

types.description: Internal result from a subcommand handler before exit code mapping. Carries both the output text and the semantic exit code.

types.name: SubcommandDispatchTable

fields.name: handlers
fields.description: Mapping of SubcommandName -> async callable(CliArgs) -> CliResult

types.description: Maps SubcommandName to its async handler function. Used by main() for dispatch. The handler signature is async (CliArgs) -> CliResult.

types.name: PactKey

validators.error_message: PACT function key must follow EXEMPLAR.CLI.<function_name> pattern

types.description: PACT attribution key embedded in public methods for Sentinel tracing.

functions.name: build_parser
functions.description: Constructs the argparse.ArgumentParser with all four subcommands (review, trust, history, adopt) and their respective arguments. Pure function with no side effects. The review subcommand accepts a positional diff-file arg plus --config and --format options. The trust subcommand has no additional args. The history subcommand accepts an optional --query flag. The adopt subcommand has no additional args. A global --debug flag is added to the parent parser.
postconditions: Returned parser has exactly 4 subcommands: review, trust, history, adopt
review subcommand has positional 'diff_file' argument
review subcommand has --config optional argument
review subcommand has --format optional argument with choices ['json', 'md', 'github']
history subcommand has --query optional argument
Parent parser has --debug flag defaulting to False
Parser prog name is 'exemplar'

functions.name: parse_and_validate_args
functions.description: Parses raw argv using the argparse parser and converts the resulting Namespace into a validated CliArgs struct. Validates that required arguments for each subcommand are present (e.g., diff_file for review). Returns structured, typed args or raises on invalid input.

inputs.name: argv
inputs.description: Raw command-line arguments. If empty list, triggers help display.

error_data.detail: argparse prints usage to stderr and raises SystemExit(2) which is caught and remapped to exit code 3

error_cases.name: missing_diff_file
error_cases.condition: review subcommand invoked without diff-file positional argument

preconditions: parser is a fully constructed ArgumentParser from build_parser()
postconditions: Returned CliArgs has a valid subcommand
If subcommand is 'review', diff_file is non-empty
output_format is a valid OutputFormat variant

functions.name: handle_review
functions.description: Async handler for the 'review' subcommand. Loads config, emits review.started Chronicler event (fire-and-forget), reads diff file, invokes intake.parse_diff → circuit.route_stages → assessor.assess → reporter.format_report pipeline, maps PipelineResult to CliResult with appropriate exit code. All output goes to stdout_output; errors and progress to stderr_output. PACT key: EXEMPLAR.CLI.handle_review.

error_cases.name: diff_file_not_found
error_cases.condition: The diff file path does not exist or is not readable

error_cases.name: diff_file_read_error
error_cases.condition: The diff file exists but cannot be read (permissions, encoding)

error_cases.name: config_load_error
error_cases.condition: Config file specified via --config does not exist or is invalid YAML/schema

error_cases.name: pipeline_stage_failure
error_cases.condition: Any pipeline stage (intake, circuit, assessor, reporter) raises an unhandled exception

error_cases.name: diff_file_empty
error_cases.condition: Diff file exists and is readable but contains zero bytes

preconditions: args.diff_file is non-empty and points to a readable file
postconditions: A review.started ChroniclerEvent was emitted (fire-and-forget, failure does not affect result)
exit_code is PASS(0), WARN(1), or BLOCK(2) on successful pipeline completion
exit_code is ERROR(3) if any pipeline stage raised an unrecoverable exception
stdout_output contains the formatted report in the requested OutputFormat
stderr_output contains diagnostic info if args.debug is true

structured_side_effects.description: Reads unified diff content from filesystem

structured_side_effects.target: config file at args.config_path or default location

structured_side_effects.description: Emits review.started ChroniclerEvent; failure is silently ignored

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is diff line count, dominated by pipeline stages

functions.name: handle_trust
functions.description: Async handler for the 'trust' subcommand. Loads config, retrieves current reviewer trust scores from assessor.get_trust_scores(), formats them as a human-readable table to stdout. Always returns exit code PASS(0) on success. PACT key: EXEMPLAR.CLI.handle_trust.

error_cases.name: trust_data_unavailable
error_cases.condition: Trust score storage is missing or corrupt

postconditions: exit_code is PASS(0) on success
exit_code is ERROR(3) on failure
stdout_output contains formatted trust score table with one row per reviewer

structured_side_effects.target: config file and trust score storage
structured_side_effects.description: Reads configuration and persisted trust scores

functions.name: handle_history
functions.description: Async handler for the 'history' subcommand. Queries past review records from Kindex store via learner.query_history(). If --query is provided, filters results by term; otherwise returns recent reviews. Formats results to stdout. PACT key: EXEMPLAR.CLI.handle_history.

error_cases.name: kindex_unavailable
error_cases.condition: Kindex store is unreachable or corrupt

error_data.detail: Failed to query review history from Kindex

postconditions: exit_code is PASS(0) on success, even if zero results returned
exit_code is ERROR(3) on failure
stdout_output contains formatted history results (may be empty set message)

structured_side_effects.description: Reads configuration and queries persisted review history

functions.name: handle_adopt
functions.description: Async handler for the 'adopt' subcommand (Cartographer). Scans cwd for linter configuration files (.eslintrc, .pylintrc, .flake8, pyproject.toml [tool.ruff], etc.) via learner.scan_linter_configs(). Reports findings with migration suggestions showing how existing rules map to Exemplar reviewers. PACT key: EXEMPLAR.CLI.handle_adopt.

error_cases.name: cwd_unreadable
error_cases.condition: Current working directory is not readable or does not exist

error_cases.name: scan_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected error during filesystem scan of linter configs

postconditions: exit_code is PASS(0) on success, even if no linter configs found
exit_code is ERROR(3) on failure
stdout_output contains scan results with per-file findings and migration suggestions

structured_side_effects.target: cwd and linter config files (.eslintrc, .pylintrc, .flake8, pyproject.toml, etc.)
structured_side_effects.description: Scans filesystem for existing linter configuration files

functions.name: build_dispatch_table
functions.description: Constructs the SubcommandDispatchTable mapping each SubcommandName to its async handler function. Pure function, returns a static mapping. Used by main() to route parsed subcommands to handlers.
postconditions: Returned table has exactly 4 entries, one per SubcommandName variant
Each handler value is an async callable accepting CliArgs and returning CliResult

functions.name: map_decision_to_exit_code
functions.description: Pure mapping function from ReviewDecision enum to CliExitCode. pass→PASS(0), warn→WARN(1), block→BLOCK(2), error→ERROR(3). Used by handle_review to convert pipeline output to process exit code.

validators.error_message: Decision must be one of: pass, warn, block, error

error_cases.name: invalid_decision
error_cases.condition: decision string is not a recognized ReviewDecision variant

preconditions: decision is a valid ReviewDecision variant
postconditions: Returned CliExitCode numeric value matches: pass→0, warn→1, block→2, error→3

functions.name: main
functions.description: Synchronous entry point for the CLI. Builds parser, parses argv (defaults to sys.argv[1:] if None), constructs dispatch table, runs the appropriate async handler via asyncio.run(), writes stdout_output to sys.stdout and stderr_output to sys.stderr, and returns the integer exit code. Catches all exceptions at the boundary and maps them to exit code 3 (ERROR). Only calls sys.exit() when invoked as __main__ script entry point — the function itself returns int for testability. PACT key: EXEMPLAR.CLI.main.

inputs.name: argv
inputs.description: Raw command-line arguments. None means use sys.argv[1:]. Empty list triggers help/error.

error_cases.name: unhandled_exception
error_cases.condition: Any exception not caught by subcommand handlers propagates to main

error_data.detail: Caught at boundary, logged to stderr, returns 3

postconditions: Return value is 0, 1, 2, or 3
On success, formatted output was written to stdout
On error, diagnostic message was written to stderr
No exception escapes this function — all are caught and mapped to exit code 3
side_effects: Calls asyncio.run() to execute async handlers
May call sys.exit() when invoked as __main__

structured_side_effects.description: Writes error diagnostics and debug output
Creates and runs asyncio event loop via asyncio.run()

invariants: CLI is a thin transport layer: it never inspects findings, computes scores, or formats report content — all logic is delegated to dependency modules
Exit codes are deterministic: given the same pipeline result, the same exit code is always returned
Chronicler event emission is fire-and-forget: failure to emit review.started never affects the pipeline result or exit code
stdout carries only subcommand output (report, trust table, history results, adopt findings); stderr carries only diagnostics and error messages
No exception escapes main(): all exceptions are caught at the boundary and mapped to CliExitCode.ERROR (3)
PACT key namespace is always 'EXEMPLAR.CLI' with function-specific suffixes
Config resolution order: --config flag > XDG_CONFIG_HOME/exemplar/.exemplar.yml > cwd/.exemplar.yml
All data models are frozen (Pydantic frozen=True); CliArgs and CliResult are immutable after construction
The --debug flag controls stderr verbosity but never changes stdout content or exit codes

data_access.rationale: Reads PUBLIC diff file content from filesystem for review pipeline input. Reads INTERNAL configuration files (YAML config, trust score storage, Kindex history store, linter config files in cwd) for pipeline parameterization and subcommand data retrieval. Writes PUBLIC formatted output (review reports, trust tables, history results, adopt findings) to stdout. Does not read or write PII — diff content may theoretically contain PII but the CLI treats it as opaque PUBLIC data passed through to pipeline stages.

side_effects.rationale: Reads unified diff content from user-specified file path to feed into the review pipeline via intake.parse_diff
Reads YAML configuration file to parameterize pipeline stages (reviewer weights, thresholds, output preferences)
Reads persisted reviewer trust scores for the trust subcommand display
Reads past review records from Kindex store for the history subcommand query
Scans cwd for .eslintrc, .pylintrc, .flake8, pyproject.toml etc. for the adopt subcommand Cartographer scan
Writes formatted subcommand results (reports, tables, scan results) to process stdout for user or CI consumption
Fire-and-forget emission of review.started ChroniclerEvent for governance audit trail; failure is silently ignored

authority.rationale: The CLI module is the sole authority for process exit code semantics (mapping ReviewDecision to OS exit codes), argparse argument definition and validation, and subcommand-to-handler dispatch routing. No other component defines CLI argument structure or exit code mappings.
